claim.observation: Volatility3 windows.netscan reports svchost.exe (PID 408) LISTENING on 0.0.0.0:3389 (TCPv4 and TCPv6), and f-response-ent (PID 328) with an ESTABLISHED TCP session 10.3.58.6:3260 -> 10.3.16.5:48769 plus LISTENING on 0.0.0.0:3260.
claim.interpretation: Remote Desktop (TCP 3389) was enabled and listening on nfury at capture time, providing a remote-access surface. The ESTABLISHED 3260 (iSCSI) session and 3260 listener belong to f-response-ent (PID 328, created 21:25:10, just before capture), i.e. the live remote forensic acquisition channel to the examiner host 10.3.16.5 - this is acquisition infrastructure, not attacker activity, and is recorded here to distinguish it from intrusion traffic.

evidence_pointer.locator: netscan offsets 0x7dbdd470 (3389 LISTEN, PID 408), 0x7f715cf0 (3260 ESTABLISHED, PID 328)
